seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Pirie West and Port Victoria. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Pirie West vs Port Victoria: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Pirie West and Port Victoria.
heading: Port Pirie West vs Port Victoria — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Pirie West, South Australia and Port Victoria, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Victoria has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Port Pirie West (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Pirie West or Port Victoria safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Pirie West has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Port Victoria scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Pirie West scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Port Victoria scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Pirie West or Port Victoria?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Victoria has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
